observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;  and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management already conducted physical count of the properties.
observations.reason:
Comparison made between the RPCI and the accounting records revealed an unreconciled difference amounting to P166,852.85.  The same is true on the reconciliation made by the Audit Team between the RPCPPE and the accounting records.  An unreconciled difference amounting to P7,830,614.54 resulted from the comparison.  These showed that the Municipality failed to conduct complete physical count of the properties. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the officials and employees ensure that the identified programs/projects are those mentioned under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and Section 287 of RA 7160.
We recommended that full implementation of these programmed projects be fast tracked/prioritized and maximized.  Ensure that all the identified priority programs/projects/activities in the Annual Investment Plan be implemented as programmed to enjoy its timely benefit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CY 2016 to the Special Trust Fund books then deposit the fund to bank account created exclusively for this purpose to be utilized to DRRM programs, projects and activities for the next five years. Draw JEV to effect the transfer.

We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the Local Government Code, Article 363 of its IRR and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint No. 01, s. of 1998 in utilizing Special Education Fund.    If necessary, cash advances for Dep-ed activities drawn from LGU funds be made by the Municipal Treasurer or designate a Special Disbursing Officer for this matter. The Local School Board (LSB) be required to prepare a balanced budget for the SEF giving priority to the much needed classroom improvements and learning facilities for a better quality of K1 to 12 program of the Dep-Ed.  Utilize fully the SEF for its intended purpose.

We recommended that management:
exert effort to expedite the completion of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan for early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003; and
identify and prioritize programs that comply with environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment.
We recommended that the management  give priority in establishing VAW Desk in every barangay to addressed concerns of violence against women.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a)	ensure that the balances posted in the financial statements must be the same with the general ledger and bank reconciliation statements to avoid production of inaccurate and misleading financial reports; and
account all differences and effect adjustment/s, if necessary.

We recommended that management refrain from drawing cash advance for IF/CF if LR of previous withdrawals remained not submitted.  A copy of credit notices or the receipt copy of liquidation report from the ICFAU, COA Chairman’s Office be furnished to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team to substantiate various credits/liquidations made for intelligence purposes and that appropriate adjustment could be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office  record all purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust this account for whatever consumption on the basis of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) which should be prepared by the requisitioning office to be submitted weekly or monthly to the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 21, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine at a given time the details and status of these accounts.  Apply the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the dormant receivable account, if there’s any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be cautious in recording transactions to avoid production of misleading financial information.  Constant monitoring of balances of all accounts in the financial statements is required and make representation with the BIR for the excess remittance, if appropriate.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-classify the accounts to its appropriate account titles by drawing journal entry voucher for fair presentation of financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the maintenance of special accounts in the general fund for economic enterprise and 20% Development Fund as mandated under Section 313 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their allotments and obligations on a monthly basis to avoid accumulation of unreconciled obligations. Reconcile the stated difference and take up adjustments, if necessary in order to produce accurate and reliable financial information.

We reiterated our recommendation that management:
Update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties were located.
Seek authority and assistance from the provincial government as well as the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notices of Delinquency and in the service of Warrants of Levy to delinquent real property owners.
We recommended strict adherence with the foregoing rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the local government unit, protect and conserve the municipality’s funds and resources by exercising prudence in utilization thereof.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to enhance the review process particularly in the processing of claims prior to payment.
We recommended that management refrain from utilizing public funds for purposes not within the dictates of good governance.

We recommended that management
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 on the specifications for the signboards, tarpaulins to be posted where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy governing public interest;
furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and
refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the persons liable to settle the NS and ND; and
ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the suspensions and disallowances for which they are liable, otherwise, the officials who issued their clearances shall be jointly liable for the unsettled disallowances of the retired employees.

We recommended that Management, through its Inventory Committee conduct complete physical count of the properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
exert more effort for timely preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements;
obtain the necessary documents to support the reconciling items identified and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of Cash, LCCA in the books of the Agency; and
maintain subsidiary ledgers of all bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose material changes in the valuation of entity’s assets to apprise the readers of the Municipality’s financial statements; and
the Municipal Assessor, as a valuer, to conform with the existing regulations and standards in valuation of property, plant and equipment.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the Agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with. As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update the payment of loan amortization for the loan availed from Municipal Development Fund Office to avoid default and imposition of the provisions of Article IX of the Subproject Loan Agreement; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the important details regarding Loans Payable and to maintain subsidiary ledgers on each loan.

We recommended that Management exert effort in determining if the National Solid Waste Management Commission approved their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.  Moreover, comply strictly with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
direct the persons concerned to prepare a Physical and Financial Plan and observe the allowable amount for confidential/intelligence fund;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry by treating the disbursement as a cash advance; and
instruct the Special Disbursing Officer to liquidate his/her cash advance and submit the liquidation report to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Auditing Unit (ICFAU) and ensure submission of complete supporting documents.
We recommended that Management utilize the SEF in pursuance to the purposes for which it was established.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Council (LDRRMC) to monitor the projects/programs/activities charged to 5% Calamity Fund especially the continuing appropriation. LDRRMFIP should also include plans for prior year’s unexpended unutilized balance of the LDRRMF so that full benefits from the fund shall be delivered to the constituents; and the
Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and maintain subsidiary ledgers for funds transferred to Trust Liability – DRRM to monitor the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the BAC to use the standard Philippine Bidding Documents, conduct all the mandatory stages required in the procurement process and observe the prescribed period of action on procurement activities; and
direct TWG to strengthen the internal control on the bid evaluation and post-qualification process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the BAC to use the standard bidding forms prescribed by the GPPB, conduct all the mandatory stages required in the procurement process and observe the prescribed period of action on procurement activities;
direct the TWG to observe the proper post-qualification process; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the requirements for all disbursements made.

We recommended that the lacking documents aforementioned be submitted to the Audit Team and compliance to pertinent rules and regulations relative to disbursements for infrastructure projects including the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly implemented.  Also, we recommended that the Acceptance and Inspection Report be properly accomplished indicating the date received and inspected to be able to determine whether to impose penalties or not.

We recommended that the lacking documents aforementioned be submitted to the Audit Team and compliance to pertinent rules and regulations relative to disbursements for infrastructure projects including the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly implemented.  We also recommended that the Acceptance and Inspection Report be properly accomplished indicating the date received and inspected to be able to determine whether to impose penalties or not.

We recommended that Management strictly follow Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM as well as  its amendments under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017  in the implementation of projects/programs/activities for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Mayor ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority projects, programs and activities that address gender issues or women’s concerns;
the GAD Focal Point System members ensure that PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget constitute gender issues and ensure proper implementation and monitoring thereof; and
the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials be guided by COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 in the disbursement of fund to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with R.A. No. 9344 especially on the budgetary requirement and submit a detailed budget for the LCPC.

1.7	We recommended that the GFPS use the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report and to attach the documents required in paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of JMC 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.

2.6	We recommended that Management include in the GPB appropriations for the attendance of its officials and employees to GAD trainings that will deepen their understanding of the GAD Program and their roles on the mainstreaming of the said program into local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and evaluation.
In case the LGU opts to attribute a portion or a whole of its budget to the GAD Budget, we also recommended that Management conduct a training program on the use of the HGDG in its GPB to assure the proper determination of the amounts that may be attributed to the GAD Budget.

3.6	We recommended that Management direct the designated MDRRMO Designated to include in the LDRRMFIP the listing of projects and activities funded by the unspent amounts of the DRRM Fund of the previous years pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure that the funds are used in the implementation of projects and activities prioritized by the LDRRMC.

4.5	We recommended that Management thoroughly study the applicability of the topics up for discussion in gatherings/conventions to the local situation before sending representatives to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenses as defined under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and to avoid the depletion of the affected fund/s.

5.8	For the deficiency noted, we recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:
Prepare the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan containing, but not limited to, the components listed in Section 17 of Republic Act No. 9003 in coordination with the various sectors within the locality;
Conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Program for the early resolution of the problems, issues and concerns encountered during its implementation; and
Conduct information and educational campaign at the barangay level with emphasis on their responsibility on solid waste segregation.

6.6	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the time frame on the submission of the accounts stipulated in the above-mentioned Section to enable our Office to communicate immediately to management deficiencies noted, if any, in the review and examination of the accounts.

7.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review and ascertain the completeness of the documents supporting claims against public funds to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.  We also recommended Management to formulate and adopt the CAPDEV Agenda to guide it in determining the interventions it needs to introduce leading to the improvement of the quality of services rendered by its personnel to its constituents.

8.5	We recommended that Management stop the practice of sending its job orders to attend trainings and seminars primarily because of the absence of employer-employee relationship between these workers and the LGU to preclude the incurrence of pecuniary loss caused by the incurrence of training expense of which the knowledge learned was not translated into improved efficiency on the delivery of basic services to its constituents.

9.6	We recommended that Management direct the Local Finance Committee to formulate the ESWM budget based on the actual needs of the program and to use financial data provided by its past years’ operations to preclude transfers of funds with no corresponding appropriation.  We also recommended that the Local Development Council prioritize the funding of developmental projects that are not operated as economic enterprise by the LGU.  Furthermore, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer NOT to process transfers of funds that are not supported with appropriation.

10.5	We recommended to stop the use of SEF for purposes other than those allowed under the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No 1.
Moreover, we recommended that the Local School Board prepare an updated budget for the Special Education Fund in the next year in accordance with the provisions of the new DILG-DEPED-DBM Joint Circular No 1 series of 2017 so that funds will be spent for the purpose it is intended.
12.6	We recommended the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials to appropriate in the LGUs Annual Budget one percent of its Internal Revenue Allotment as required by RA 9344.

13.7	We recommended that management minimize the appropriation on celebrations to what is really necessary to enable it to formulate sound financial plans with the end view of achieving its goals and objectives.

14.4	We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly scrutinize the documents supporting the claims charged to LGU funds to ensure their completeness to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
We commended management for their continuous effort in undertaking these projects and activities for the improvement and development of municipality for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Continuous implementation of various projects sourced from BUB and 20% development funds.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to fully implement and fast track the implementation of identified development projects listed in its Annual Investment Plan (AIP).  Any revision/s shall be properly justified and to be included in the succeeding Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016 only two projects were not totally implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that management:

Update and post notice of delinquency for property taxes at the main entrance of the municipal building and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay where these properties were located.

Seek authority and assistance from the provincial government as well as the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notices of Delinquency and in the service of Warrants of Levy to delinquent real property owners.
observations.managementAction: Request for assistance from the province has already undertaken for these delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 18.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan be required to submit these afore-cited documents to substantiate their claim for RATA, otherwise Notice Suspension or Disallowance shall be issued on their claims for RATA.
observations.managementAction: Effective payroll for April 2016, computation of RATA of the Sanggunian were based from the actual attendance of the SB Members in session.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds and property and be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-003 for the prevention of IUEE.  Thus, we encourage the concerned officials to observe DILG Memorandum Circular No. 11 – 59 dated April 25, 2011.

We have recommended that management require immediate settlement of the audit disallowances and suspensions, and strict compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the disbursement of its funds to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions in the future.
observations.managementAction: A total of ₱50,000.00 was refunded from the disallowed calamity assistance granted to officials and employees.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 28

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;  and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The agency conducted a physical inventory of its properties.
observations.reason:
Management commented that the inventory report was not yet completed due to lack of time and manpower.  Also, the Head of the inventory committee chair was also busy with the BAC responsibilities and other treasury functions. 
Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with. As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

We also recommended strict compliance with the general guidelines and documentary requirements for the salary of casual personnel provided under Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the hiring of casuals upon assumption of the new Local Chief Executive.
observations.reason:
Management commented that due to lower income of the Municipality from previous years, they were not able to escape the excess of the PS cost over PS limitations.
Reiterated in Finding No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: For Cy 2016 purchases, the Accounting Office initially recorded the supplies and materials as inventories.
All collections were deposited as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Missing official receipts reported in CY 2015 were not yet submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding ledgers and exert extra effort to secure pertinent documents that will support the adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Due from and Due to Other Funds Accounts were already reconciled as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant was advised to give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 70, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Management exerted time and effort in the timely submission of accounts and reports thus, greatly reducing the delay.
observations.reason: Management commented that the laxity of other departments, particularly the Treasurer’s Office, sometimes caused the delay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the proper appropriation of five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources as its LDRRM Fund to support disaster risk management

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, submit the recommendation from the LDRRMO of the municipality to transfer said funds to other LDRRMCs and declaration of state of calamity in support to the above mentioned disbursements. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify each transaction so that it will be recorded in their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipality properly appropriated 5% of its estimated revenue from regular sources.
observations.reason:
The Accounting Office still failed to disclose in the Notes to FS the amounts and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2016. 
Reiterated in Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20%Development Fund and their implementation. See to it also that those development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Projects for CY 2015 were fully implemented as December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: There are projects which are not yet fully implemented for CY 2016 due to limited manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue a Memorandum compelling all accountable officers with cash shortages   fully the missing funds, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges, as the evidence may warrant.  Further, relieve and assign these personnel to functions which do not involve cash accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of memorandum order to all accountable Officers with cash shortages to transfer assignments and to function which do not involve cash accountabilities
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require intact and daily remittance of collections from all collectors and to refrain from issuing additional stubs of accountable forms unless collections from the previously issued accountable forms have been remitted.
observations.managementAction: Strictly require all revenue collectors to prepare RCD daily and all accountable forms currently issued be presented upon remittance of collections for verification
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all Accountable Officers, including the Municipal Treasurer to certify monthly the ending balances in their cashbook in conformity with the provisions of the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: Require all Revenue Collectors including the Mun. Treasurer to post all monthly collections in their cashbooks subject to be certified by the Mun. Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineering Office to review the reasonableness of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) on the on-going procurements and adhere to the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 22 Series of 2015 for upcoming projects to judiciously determine the ABC beneficial to the government.
